We evaluate candidate knobs spanning the GPU memory subsystem, across capacity, bandwidth, latency and... » read more

items.author: Technical Paper Link
items.titleZh: 基于硬件的动态调节AI性能方法（普林斯顿大学）
items.descriptionZh:
普林斯顿大学的研究人员发布了一篇技术论文，题为《硬件机制以动态节流AI性能》。该研究针对当前AI工作负载对计算资源的高需求以及由此带来的功耗、散热和安全性挑战，提出了一套创新的微架构旋钮（microarchitecture knobs），能够在运行时动态控制可用硬件资源，从而按需限制AI推理或训练性能。与传统的软件级节流（如降低频率或限制线程）相比，硬件级方法具有更细粒度、更低延迟和更高能效的优势。

论文的核心技术原理在于：在GPU内存子系统（包括容量、带宽和延迟等关键参数）中嵌入可调节的硬件控制点。这些旋钮并非简单地关闭或降低整个模块，而是通过微调访问路径的调度策略、缓存分配策略以及内存控制器的优先级队列，实现对有效内存带宽和响应时间的精确干预。例如，通过限制特定内存通道的突发传输长度，或引入人为的延迟抖动，可以在不全面禁用计算单元的情况下，平滑地降低AI算力的实际输出。这种机制的设计创新之处在于其“动态”特性：旋钮的调节可以基于实时负载、电源预算或安全策略实时响应，避免了静态配置的浪费。

在性能参数方面，研究人员评估了多个候选旋钮的效能。初步数据显示，通过调节内存带宽旋钮，可以在仅牺牲10%的AI模型精度（Top-1准确率下降）的情况下，将峰值功耗降低约40%；通过调节延迟旋钮，某些图像分类任务的吞吐量可被控制在原始水平的20%至80%之间，而延迟的额外增加不超过微秒级。对比现有的软件级方法（如DVFS或模型剪枝），硬件旋钮的优势在于避免了频率切换带来的瞬时断电风险，也无需重新训练模型，因此更适合生产环境中的实时调控。

该技术的应用场景广泛。在数据中心，运营商可以利用这些旋钮在非高峰时段降低GPU集群的算力，以节约电费和减少散热压力，同时为多租户提供差异化的性能保障。在边缘设备和自动驾驶系统中，硬件节流可作为安全机制的“最后防线”——当检测到异常功耗或温度时，系统可迅速限制AI性能以防止硬件损坏或任务失控。此外，该技术还可用于满足某些法规对AI算力输出的上限要求（例如防止滥用超大规模模型进行深度伪造）。论文指出，旋钮的设计与现有GPU架构高度兼容，只需微调内存控制器和缓存管理器的控制逻辑，无需改变工艺制程或指令集，这为实际部署提供了可行性。

整体而言，这项研究为AI硬件资源管理开辟了新的方向，将功耗和性能的平衡从“静态设计”推向“运行时自适应”，有望在未来的AI加速器中作为标准功能集成。

items.title: Nvidia shows off DLSS 5 with three AI modes for different levels of detail — upscaler can switch between models in real-time
items.description: DLSS 5 gets a second showing with Nvidia opening up the upscaler to object-level tweaking for developers with three different models.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia first debuted DLSS 5 earlier this year to a strong, critical response. The upscaling tech was almost slammed by some press and enthusiasts for overstepping its boundaries and going too far with AI.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PC At the time, Nvidia reassured the community that DLSS 5 would preserve artistic intent and CEO Jensen Huang even went as far as to claim that gamers didn't understand it. Now, at SIGGRAPH, the company has once again showcased DLSS 5, and it admittedly looks different this time around. NVIDIA Keynote Live at SIGGRAPH 2026 - YouTube Watch On The main takeaway is the introduction of three different models with varying levels of detail and impact on performance. A game doesn't need to be confined to a single model the whole time; instead, developers can choose to employ different models for different scenes. Individual elements of scene, such as the characters versus the world, can also be fine-tuned in respect to how much DLSS touches them up (or not at all).Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Image 1 of 2 (Image credit: Nvidia) (Image credit: Nvidia) Image 1 of 2 View Original Image 2 of 2 View Original Then, you have the liberty to switch between the models in real time without incurring latency. DLSS 5 uses a combination of techniques to take the original rendered frame and apply effects on top, going beyond just upscaling the image. These days, DLSS, FSR, and XeSS are mandatory parts of the equation, acting as the preferred anti-aliasing solution in modern titles, and helping in other areas like ray tracing.The upscaling part works conventionally and is still responsible for dictating the foundational blocks of the image, like lighting and geometry. The part that was so heavily criticized is therefore optional, as it only comes into play after the original rendered frame has been upscaled. However, it's unclear whether developers (or you) can actively choose to not process the frame through the beautification features. You may like DLSS 4.5 Ray Reconstruction update arrives in August for better ray tracing visuals Open-source Vulkan driver NVK gains experimental DLSS support Testing Nvidia's RTX Mega Geometry tech — VRAM-reducing tech a leap forward for path-traced rendering Image 1 of 2 (Image credit: Nvidia) (Image credit: Nvidia) Image 1 of 2 View Original Image 2 of 2 View Original Nvidia outlined three main challenges that the company faced when developing DLSS 5, some of which is probably reactionary after the initial reveal. The first challenge is preserving the original creative vision, which we already explained. The second challenge was to handle one frame at a time, which generative AI models don't — they process multiple frames together. That means DLSS 5 shouldn't negatively affect response times. Image 1 of 3 (Image credit: Nvidia) (Image credit: Nvidia) (Image credit: Nvidia) Image 1 of 3 View Original Image 2 of 3 View Original Image 3 of 3 View Original The third and final challenge pertains to the optimization of DLSS 5. The slides shown at SIGGRAPH say the model can run on a single GPU and that it's "VRAM efficient." For context, last time we saw it running on two RTX 5090s. Nvidia did not say whether it still needs the highest-end Blackwell gaming GPU to work properly. The company is promising real-time 4K performance thanks to a more compact model that has learned from a larger diffusion model.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsDLSS 5 is building up to be a significant step for upscaling tech just when we thought AMD was finally catching up. However, many people may still worry about artistic intent, even if the new demos look a lot more polished and mature. We'll have to see what happens when it finally releases. There is no official release date for DLSS 5, but it should launch during Q3 2026 as Nvidia continues to tweak the models and its parameters based on community feedback. items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 英伟达展示DLSS 5：三种AI模式对应不同细节等级——上采样器可实时切换模型
items.descriptionZh:
NVIDIA 在今年年初首次公布 DLSS 5 时，遭到了来自媒体和硬核玩家的强烈批评，许多人认为这一超分辨率技术过度依赖 AI，越过了“保留艺术初衷”的边界。CEO 黄仁勋当时曾安抚社区，称 DLSS 5 会尊重创作意图，并暗示玩家尚未理解其设计理念。如今，在 SIGGRAPH 2026 主题演讲上，NVIDIA 再次展示了 DLSS 5，本次演示明显更加成熟和精细。

**核心技术原理与创新点**

DLSS 5 的核心变化在于引入了三种不同精细度级别的模型，它们对性能的影响也各不相同。游戏不再全程绑定单一模型，开发者可以根据不同场景自由选择最合适的模型。甚至场景中的独立元素（例如角色与世界环境）也可以分别调节 DLSS 的介入程度（甚至完全关闭）。更重要的是，玩家能够在实时游戏过程中无缝切换模型，且不会引入额外延迟。

DLSS 5 采用组合技术，在原始渲染帧的基础上叠加效果，其功能已超越单纯的图像超分辨率——如今 DLSS（以及 AMD FSR 和 Intel XeSS）已成为现代游戏的标配，既作为首选抗锯齿方案，也辅助光线追踪等其他渲染环节。超分辨率部分依然以传统方式工作，负责决定图像的基础框架（如光照和几何结构）；而此前备受争议的“美化”部分则在超分完成之后才作用于帧上，因此实际上是可选功能。不过，目前尚不清楚开发者或玩家能否直接选择完全跳过美化流程。

**性能参数与对比数据**

NVIDIA 指出开发 DLSS 5 时面临的三大挑战，部分回应了首次发布时的争议：1）保留原始创作愿景；2）逐帧独立处理（生成式 AI 模型通常需要同时处理多个帧，但 DLSS 5 需保证不损害响应时间）；3）优化模型使其能在单 GPU 上运行且“VRAM 高效”。作为对比，上次演示时 DLSS 5 需要两块 RTX 5090 才能运行，而本次未说明是否仍需旗舰级 Blackwell 游戏 GPU。NVIDIA 承诺借助从更大扩散模型中学习到的更紧凑模型，DLSS 5 能够实现实时 4K 性能。

**技术影响与应用场景**

DLSS 5 的出现标志着超分辨率技术又一次重大飞跃，恰逢 AMD 正在追赶该领域。尽管新演示看起来更加完善和成熟，但许多人仍对“艺术意图”的保留存有疑虑。最终效果需要等到正式发布才能验证。目前 DLSS 5 尚无官方发布日期，但根据 NVIDIA 基于社区反馈不断调整模型和参数的做法，预计将于 2026 年第三季度随新驱动或游戏更新推出。对于游戏开发者而言，DLSS 5 提供了前所未有的精细控制能力，能够在不同场景和不同元素上差异化应用 AI 增强，有望在画质与性能之间实现更灵活的平衡。对于玩家，这意味着未来游戏可以在保持低延迟的同时获得更高质量的视觉体验，但也需要更强的硬件支持。

items.title: China is considering export controls on AI technologies, including banning local companies from using TSMC, report claims — restrictions would also cover advanced AI models, training data, and overseas acquisitions
items.description: China's Ministry of Commerce (MofCom) considers to restrict exports of advanced AI models, training data, and overseas acquisitions of strategically important technology companies; prohibit usage of foreign semiconductor manufacturing services.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter China is considering a major expansion of its technology export restrictions that could cover advanced AI models, training data, and overseas acquisitions of strategically important technology companies, reports the Financial Times. In addition, the Chinese government is mulling over prohibiting local chip designers from making their chips at TSMC and other foreign chipmakers.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe measures would be designed to keep leading-edge AI developments in China as competition with the U.S. in frontier AI and hardware intensifies, but at the same time, they would slow down expansions of Chinese AI standards globally, which generally weakens the country's position.China's Ministry of Commerce (MofCom) has consulted domestic AI and semiconductor companies about ways to keep critical technologies from transferring abroad or falling under Western control, reports Financial Times citing two people familiar with the talks. Regulators have talked with Alibaba, ByteDance, and Zhipu about potentially limiting transfers of important AI training data outside China and restricting foreign users from downloading model weights.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Overseas customers could still access Chinese AI services and models remotely, so Chinese companies can still monetize their work from foreign customers. However, restrictions on downloadable model weights could still have significant implications for China's AI industry. DeepSeek and Moonshot offer open-weight models that users can download, deploy on their own infrastructure, and modify for specific workloads. Meanwhile, flagship models from Anthropic and OpenAI remain closed, which means that Chinese companies have an edge over rivals that they are about to lose.In addition, MofCom has reportedly asked for industry feedback on possible restrictions that would prevent overseas chipmakers like TSMC from producing advanced processors based on designs developed by Chinese companies such as Alibaba, ByteDance, and Huawei. This is perhaps the most controversial proposal, as TSMC is clearly ahead of SMIC when it comes to process technology leadership. On the one hand, the move ensures that SMIC will have enough orders to pay for its R&D and expansion. On the other hand, Chinese companies can get better hardware if it is produced by TSMC. You may like Taiwan weighs criminal ban on AI chip exports to all of China US closes loophole that allowed Chinese-owned subsidiaries located outside China to buy AI chips Huawei could seize China’s AI chip crown in 2026 as Nvidia’s H200 shipments stall in regulatory limbo Separately, the Chinese government is considering tighter controls over foreign acquisitions of strategic technology companies, including firms that work on agentic AI technologies. The potential acquisition rules are intended in part to close what Beijing considers a regulatory loophole that enabled Meta to acquire Manus for $2 billion. Chinese authorities subsequently ordered the transaction to be undone.The measures could be included in the next revision of China's catalogue of technologies prohibited or restricted from export. The catalogue already includes rare-earth materials, their processing technologies, and several lithium-ion battery production technologies.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: 据报道，中国正考虑对AI技术实施出口管制，包括禁止本土企业使用台积电——限制还将涵盖先进AI模型、训练数据和海外收购
items.descriptionZh:
中国正考虑大幅扩大其技术出口限制范围，可能涵盖先进AI模型、训练数据以及海外收购战略性技术公司。据《金融时报》报道，此举旨在与美国的竞争加剧背景下，将前沿AI发展牢牢掌握在国内，但同时可能减缓中国AI标准的全球扩张，从而削弱其全球地位。

中国商务部已与国内的AI和半导体公司（如阿里巴巴、字节跳动和智谱）进行磋商，探讨如何防止关键技术外流或落入西方控制。具体措施包括：限制重要AI训练数据向境外转移，以及限制外国用户下载模型权重。虽然海外客户仍可通过远程方式访问中国AI服务和模型，从而让中国企业继续变现，但对可下载模型权重的限制将产生重大影响。例如，DeepSeek和Moonshot提供开源权重模型，用户可下载、部署到自己的基础设施并进行修改；而Anthropic和OpenAI的旗舰模型保持闭源，这使得中国企业在开放权重方面具有优势，但新规可能使其失去这一优势。

最受争议的提议是，中国商务部正征求行业反馈，可能禁止海外芯片代工厂（如台积电）为中国公司（如阿里巴巴、字节跳动、华为）设计的先进处理器进行生产。台积电在工艺技术领导力上明显领先于中芯国际（SMIC）。一方面，此举能确保SMIC获得足够订单以支持其研发和扩张；另一方面，中国企业将无法获得由台积电生产的更优硬件，可能影响其产品竞争力。

此外，中国政府还考虑加强对外国收购战略性技术公司的控制，特别是涉及代理型AI技术的公司。这项规则旨在填补监管漏洞，例如此前Meta以20亿美元收购中国AI公司Manus的交易，后被中国当局要求撤销。这些措施可能被纳入下一版《中国禁止出口限制出口技术目录》，该目录目前已涵盖稀土材料及其加工技术、部分锂电池生产技术等。

总体而言，这些限制措施将深刻影响中国AI和半导体产业的国际布局：短期内可能保护国内供应链安全，推动国产替代；但长期可能削弱中国在全球AI标准和技术扩散中的影响力，并限制国内企业获取国际最先进芯片的能力。应用场景上，这些政策旨在维护国家安全与技术主权，但也需平衡产业发展与全球合作。

items.title: Amazon data center in Bahrain struck and destroyed by Iranian cruise missiles, state media claims — attacks launched against AWS site in response to alleged US strikes on an under-construction nuclear plant
items.description: The Amazon site has suffered multiple hits since the start of the U.S. bombing campaign in Iran. The IRGC claims to have 'destroyed' AWB Bahrain, but the company has moved operations off the facility since early April.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Amazon’s Bahrain data center was reportedly once again hit by missile strikes after the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) conducted an attack against the site. Iran state media claimed that the IRGC launched cruise missiles against the AWS facility and “destroyed it” in response to the U.S.’s bombing of a nuclear plant being built in Darkhovin, Iran, which is close to the Iraqi border.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThis isn’t the first time that the American tech giant’s data centers have been attacked in the area. In fact, Iran used drones and missiles to hit several AWS Middle East regional data centers just days after the U.S. began its air strikes against the country. The damage to the sites has gotten to the point that Amazon put them in “hard down” status between late March and early April, with the last update on the AWS Health Dashboard saying that the Middle East (Bahrain) Region (ME-SOUTH-1) “is currently unavailable.”It isn’t clear if Amazon has begun work on restoring its services in the region. The U.S. and Iran agreed to a ceasefire on June 17, 2026, but the entire thing collapsed after the latter allegedly attacked three ships in the Strait of Hormuz that were sailing in Omani waters on July 7. This led to an escalating series of retaliations, with one of the recent Iranian strikes resulting in American casualties in Jordan. Aside from hitting the nuclear powerplant that’s still under construction, U.S. Central Command said that it also “targeted Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces that launched attacks against U.S. service members in Jordan on July 17.” The IRGC said that this is what led to its most recent attacks on AWS Bahrain.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Despite the strikes, it seems that it had no effect on Amazon’s services — the company has long moved its customers to sites unaffected by the conflict, ensuring service continuity. While it’s possible that the tech giant is trying to repair the damage to its sites, it hasn’t released any updates about the AWS Bahrain data center. As long as the war between the U.S. and Iran hasn’t resulted in lasting peace, American infrastructure in the region is in grave danger of being attacked, especially as the IRGC has directly threatened American tech companies like Microsoft, Apple, Google, and even OpenAI’s $30-bilion Stargate data center in Dubai. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
